title:
Commentary on Membership
(Members data presented are as of December 31, 2026)
content:
The credit union maintained stable membership headcount during the period under review.
(As of December 31, 2026) Current members totaled 44 thousand, reflecting no change year-over-year and no quarter-over-quarter movement. Potential membership remained flat at 156 thousand.

Members per full-time employee exhibited a declining trend through Q1'26 relative to the prior period shown on the chart. This indicates that staffing levels have grown faster than service demand from existing members, suggesting potential overstaffing or misalignment between workforce capacity and member engagement needs.

Shares per member rose in Q1'26 compared with YE'25. Existing members hold a larger average deposit balance at year-end 2026 relative to the prior period shown on the chart, which strengthens the funding base per member even though total membership count remained unchanged during this reporting window.

The credit union’s stable headcount combined with rising shares-per-member and declining efficiency metrics suggests that while deposit depth is improving, staffing alignment requires attention. Continued focus on targeted engagement to retain existing members will support sustained growth in average balances without requiring additional staff.
